data.title: As a developer, reviewers verify external references exist and flag hallucinated code
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add explicit hallucination verification instructions to standard and deep-tier reviewer checklists so reviewers verify that external API calls, model names, and library function references actually exist.
**Why**: Hallucinated references (nonexistent API parameters, invalid model names, fabricated library functions) currently pass review undetected and get committed.
**Scope**:
- IN: Correctness checklist updates for code-reviewer-standard.md, code-reviewer-deep-correctness.md, reviewer-base.md schema example update to include fragile severity
- OUT: ESCALATE_REVIEW signal (Story 3). Classifier changes (Story 5).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the standard and deep-tier correctness checklists contain explicit steps to verify that external API calls, model names, and library function references in the diff exist
  ← Satisfies: "Review sub-agents at standard tier and above verify that external API calls, model names, and library function references in the diff actually exist"
- When this story is complete, the verification instructions clearly distinguish internal project APIs (verify via Grep/Read in the repo) from external library APIs (verify via import source or documentation)
  ← Satisfies: "Review sub-agents at standard tier and above verify that external API calls, model names, and library function references in the diff actually exist"
- When this story is complete, unverifiable references are flagged as fragile (high confidence it does not exist) or important (moderate confidence) findings
  ← Satisfies: "flag unverifiable references as findings"
- When this story is complete, the reviewer-base.md schema example block includes fragile as a valid severity value alongside critical, important, and minor
  ← Satisfies: "A new finding severity fragile is added"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Adversarial] reviewer-base.md schema example must be updated to include fragile — reviewers use this as their format reference and will not emit fragile without seeing it in the schema
- [Reliability] Hallucination verification triggers false positives on internal APIs if the checklist does not clearly distinguish internal vs external — include explicit guidance on what to verify and what to skip

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
